div1.entryFree:
Honourably, 1) as it becomes a man of honour: the noble lord most h. doth uphold his word, LLL V, 2, 449.
2) with tokens of respect: use her h. H6C III, 2, 123 (F1 honourable). H6C III, 2, 123 h. received him, H8 IV, 2, 19. ordered h. Caes. V, 5, 79.
3) decently, becomingly: art not ashamed? Of what, lady? of speaking h.? Ado III, 4, 29. do this message h. Tit. IV, 4, 104.
